Counter: One of the best counter team requires huge whale investments but you can still make decent counter team with Caecus. If you have a Protoparadise can pick up Nautila for the counter duo. 
Tentacle: Anyone in Aequor basically interacts with tentacles. 
- There are 2 main teams: Lemurian (Fauxborn) team and a discard comp with the core being Corposant and Aurita. 
- I would recommend getting at least 1 copy of Corpo to enable the discard comp and get at least 2 dupes for E2 (3 copies)

But then who do I pair with them is the question

Miryiam E2 or Winkle E0 is a good battery for both, Castor and Fauxborn. Miryiam needs E2 to play her 3 cost battery(Gains Retain + Prepare), where Winkle battery function is on her Exalt. Miryiam is better for the fact she is a Lemurian for Fauxborn Passive, but worse for the fact she wants E2. A plus side to Winkle is she has Weakness App on 2 cost, where Miryiam has Weakness + Vuln on her Rouse. 

Castor likes Horla/Erica/Clem/Pollux(if Pollux main DPS).

Fauxborn likes Tulu/Goliath/Faros/Miryiam, Lemurians. Murphy is amazing for sacrifice management as well.

Keep in mind you want a Keyflare Awakener for every team(Clem/Erica/Murphy/Fauxborn/Miryiam).

Castor can be a Support/Warden at E0, and DPS at E3. Fauxborn needs E3 for DPS, and E0 is Support(Keyflare). 

There are broken Supports that can be slotted into nearly any team.

Chaos: Hameln
Caro: Thais/Aigis
Ultra: Clem/Horla

There is additional information, I probably forgot, but *eh~*

Truthfully, I have no clue. Have I seen Corpo and Fauxborn together? Yes, but I’ve never seen Aurita with them. Another issue with this team is it your only Vuln + Weakness if on Murphy 3 cost. Corposant wants E2 for Warden, and E3 for DPS. Fauxborn wants E3 for DPS. Thus leaving Aurita as DPS in this comp which is… unreliable….

If you are going to run this comp, I would give:

Corposant: 
CoV: Burial Grounds
WoDs: Anything that gives Death Resistance/Anything that gives Shield Amp (Dusk and Dawn is amazing for Death Res, and Keyflare on EoT)

Aurita:
CoV: Crimsum Pulse
WoD: Crit Rate WoDs for 100% CR with Crimsum Pulse activated(Special Training as Golden WoD)

Murphy:
CoV: Life Drain
WoDs: Fauxborn Sig WoD + Keyflare WoDs

Fauxborn:
CoV: Medicine
WoDs: Titans Blade + Dearest Friend

You can try this, but I’ll be real hard without a proper damage dealer.

The Hameln/Thais/Doreslain/Kath team will hit a wall eventually, but I say keep enjoying the team until you can’t anymore~ <:EliteBagels:1439384070487408722> 

You’re only a week old so it’s normal to have under level Awakeners.

Fauxborn is seen more with Lemurians and Tent Scaling, but the Castor one works too. Castor just works with anything, and everything. 

Don’t feel rushed, take your time and enjoy the game at your own pace~

As stated above, your DPS can be Corpo or Fauxborn. However, they both need E3 to perform. Aurita needs **ALOT** more investment to become a DPS(Potrait as well). Tulu isn’t really a DPS, and at OE, he is a OverExalt Bot that exists to never play his cards, solely proc “Aftershock” with Discard Mechanic.

Mhm~

Kath is played mainly in Mono Chaos or Ultra Chaos. Kath teams mainly consist of 3 supports and a Kath. This being because Kath, when built well enough, correctly and properly, can be a DPS/Nuked/Warden/STR Stacker all by herself. 

Chaos: 
Budget: Ramona/Ogier/Karen(E0/E3/OE)
Invest: TRamona/Ryker(E0/E1)/Hameln(E0)

Ultra:
Budget: Tinct(E0/E3/OE)/Winkle(E0/E3/OE)/Inferno(OE)
Invest: Horla(E1)/Clem(E0/E3/OE)

CoVs: Feast from Afar / Paradox

WoDs:
SSR Limited: 
Tawil Sig/Horla Sig/Kath Sig/Pollux Sig
SSR Standard:
Goliath Sig/Alva Sig

SR: “Land of Nonexistence” and “Dearest Friend”

In the Side Story Tab, there is a “Ramona” with a blindfold. Unlock the side story via “Reality Verges” and complete the side story. You can a feather item. With the item + 60 Standard Pulls, you unlock Genesis Ramona - Timeworn Ramona. Timeworn Ramona and Base Ramona share a “Awakener Slot” compared to other “Genesis Forms” such as Fauxborn and Murphy, NOT SHARING a “Awakener Slot” and being able to exist on the same team.

Thank you ❤️ And how many teams do we need for end game content may I ask?

Kath wants E3 to actually stack Fiamma smoothly, while having a scaling factor for her damage. Clem getting E3 would be *nice*.

Same issue as someone above, Corpo wants E2 to be a Warden, E3 for DPS. Fauxborn is Support until E2/E3. Both being E0, make them both function as Semi-Supports. Aurita wants E2 to deal any form of damage, and OE + A ton of investment for Aurita to be a respectable main damage source.

For Endgame Story, you will probably run 2-3 teams. For Endgame Game Mode(D-Tide), 5 Teams. 

For literally the last content in the game, 10 Teams.

Aequor Mechanics - Tentacles:

Blue Stance: Dmg + Gain 1 Tent Next Turn
White Stance: Shield + Less Tent Dmg This Turn
Red Stance: Tent Slap every Dmg Instance + More Tent Dmg + Lose 1 Tent at the start of Next Turn


Caro Mechanic - Crimsum Furnace - Embryo Meter:

Crimsum Furance increases at the beginning if every turn or on Salvador Cards. Crimsum furnace is the little red orb that can clicked to heal that number of HP. Embryo meter increases every start of the turn or on Thais/Salva Cards, and the increase scales with missing HP. At 100% you generate a “Embryo”. That Embryo has 2 purposes. One is to eat it for 30 Aliemus and a 10% Team Wide Crit Rate buff this turn. Second is to have a Caro Awakener, “Devour” it by using their Exalts, removing the “Embryo” from hand, snd providing additional effects to the Exalt.

And murphy

Celeste:
Celeste Exalt is a revive that can be used once every encounter or every 6 turns at E3. A fail safe. Her 3 cost applies Weakness and heals you a tad. Her 0 cost is 1 Extra Arithma and a tad bit of healing. Her 3 cost has Retain(At E2), and 0 cost has Retain, both used when needed and held in hand.

Thais:
Thais 2 cost is a battery to accelerate herself and Cantena to Exalt quicker and more often. Her other 2 cost generates Skey and Embryo Charge, and allows you to choose between Vuln/Weakness/Embryo Gen. Very flexible debuffs. Her Rouse gives you the ability to generate “Scions” which have 2x the effect of normal embryos, providing even more Aliemus on use, and “Devour” effects on Caro Awakeners proc twice. Her Exalt allows her to dupe a Awakeners Cards, in this case, Cantena’s “Bloodthirsty Flails” on Burst Turn. Devour effect gives Aliemus to all Awakeners, which is *eh*.

Salvador:
Salvador has a 3 cost that applies Weakness, and generates Crimson Furnace and Embryo Meter. His 2 cost is flexible w/ Retain, allowing you to use it when you need it. His 2 cost has three options, left consuming your “Crimsum Furnace” and giving a shield based on the amount consumed, middle consumes your “Crimsum Furnace” and drops a nuke depending on amount consumed, right consumes your HP to gain “Crimsum Furnace”. Salva is a support in this case, so you want to hold his 2 cost w/ left option to Shield yourself most of the time. His Rouse is 3 cost, and gives a 0 cost Retain Card, granting 1 Temp Barrier and generating 1 Embryo instantly. Salvador’s Exalt applies Vuln to all enemies, and “Devour” gives your team Crit Rate and Temp Str which is strong for burst turns.

Her playstyle is use waltz to scale the team including tulu. Then on burst turn you change to red stance and exalt

Cantena:
Her 1 cost AOE + STR gain can be played whenever. Her 1 cost “Crimsum Shackles” without Retain, three procs, into a 4 cost called “Bloodthristy Flail” w/ Retain and Prepare. Thus you want to play her “Crimsum Shackles” to gain “Bloodthristy Flail” as soon as possible to start “Preparing” them. “Crimsum Shackles” reduce your HP, but “Bloodthristy Flails” heal based on missing HP. Her Rouse gives another “Crimsum Shackle”, and gives all Cantena’s Cards Retain, and scales her “Crimsum Shackle” and “Bloodthristy Flail” Dmg over time(turns). Her Exalt, for burst turn with Thais Dupe, draws Cantena cards and reduces them by 1, and “Scions” make this draw more Cantena cards and reduce them by 2.

Posse: Lone Seed is used to duplicate Flails, not Shackles. Flails has Retain and Prepare, so they can be held in hand and reduced cost over turns for Burst Turn.

Standard Burst turn is:
Shackles into Flails -> Posse Dupe Flail -> Survive to next turn -> Repeat or Go Forward -> Go Forward means Posse Dupe Flail -> Salvador Exalt(Optional) -> Into Thais Dupe Flail -> Cantena Exalt -> Play Cantena Strike into 1 Cost(Hatred Unleash) -> Flails -> Cantena Defend

Due to “Crimsum Furnace”, and Celeste tads of healing, and Cantena’s “Bloodthirsty Flails” for chunks of healing, this basically negates bleed. Bleed is reduced by  2x the amount healed(Heal 49, reduce Bleed by 98)

I have these 2 stages and teams left

your Jenkin team has a mix of ideas.

Jenkin + Muphy = discard and raising tent power and flipping to redstance then hit with mouse swarm. Normally add Corpo or Tulu or Cassiah or...

Jenkin + Castor = Hold onto cards and exalt jenkin for big burst turn. You exalt jenkin to give Castor big boost damage with cards you held onto.
<@693983290423509042> hi sry i have never used castor so wanted to understand how he works first
Like what cards to hold onto
What cards to use and aim for

Castor is kinda easy. 1st you use any of his non plume card at start of turn to generate more plumes. Then you use 1 plume for corrosion. Then allies do tons of damage. Spare plumes stay in hand for shield and future turns

Casor = Rouse him now all his hits crit. And he always add corrosion with his plumes. Non rouse he only adds corrosion with first plume.

His plumes you want to stack in your deck - so you play quasar (his card first in the turn) to put more plumes in deck.

Hold onto the plumes in your hand at end of turn he adds sheild.

Burst turn - play those plumes and load up corrosion onto enemy (nice part is they don't add to harmony) The more corrosion Castor applied that turn the more damage his other cards will do. 

Once you have a lot of corrosion on an enemy, you do big hits with the other units to try and use up all that corrosion.

unit tier lists are really tricky as you look into game more: you are not picking just 1 unit, you are picking a whole style and lineup eventually. So I'd say that first you need to decide which team you wanna play and then go for that, rather than just getting strong units in general (I mean you will do that too eventually as you start building more teams for d-tide)
and to decide team - check replays in game or youtube. Thankfully there is a new feature since anniversary that you can check story and d-tide replays of other's recent clears
